From: noc-team@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Network Operations Centre — Monitoring Tooling Question
Date: March 7, 2026

Hi Alice,

During our review of the Site A migration runbook, our NOC team raised
a question that we wanted to flag to the project.

Currently, the NOC uses SolarWinds NPM for network monitoring across all
GC infrastructure. As part of the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment, we
will need to ensure that the new switches are properly onboarded into
the SolarWinds platform with appropriate polling intervals and alert
thresholds.

However, our NOC manager has also flagged that SolarWinds' support for
the Catalyst 9500 platform requires upgrading the NPM module to version
2024.1, which we are not currently running. This upgrade involves changes
to the SolarWinds configuration and database schema.

Question: Should the NPM upgrade be included as part of the NGIS Phase 3
network migration activities? Or should it be handled separately?

We want to flag this because:
- If we do it as part of NGIS Phase 3: the switches will be properly
  monitored from day one, but it involves changes to NOC tooling
  (which might be considered "network monitoring tool upgrades").
- If we do it separately: we risk a monitoring gap during the initial
  post-migration period.

We defer to the project team's judgment on how to classify this work.

NOC Team, SSC Infrastructure Operations
